pages.full_text:
We motored inland past Sutton Hoo & Felixstowe, over the Orwell River to camp at __Bentley__. A quiet fold.

__Monday__
On to the Naze, built in 1700s. Naze Tower, to help shipping before light houses. A nice art/ craft exhibit on all the floors. Spectacular view from tower to the Ness. Hoardes of school children Nice receptionist On to Frinton after lunch. We wandered near the keys & Graces Road looking at residential

[Photo of Walton-on-the-Naze with Naze Tower on cliff edge; caption printed on photo: "Walton-on-the-Naze"]

"Mermaid" by Duffy Tomei. Actually the Naze Tower
photo_captions: "Mermaid" by Duffy Tomei. Actually the Naze Tower
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation from a previous spread. 'keys' may read 'Keys' as a place name (the Quays?) — could be 'Quays' misread or local road name. 'Hoardes' is diary's own spelling. Photo caption printed on image reads 'Walton-on-the-Naze'; handwritten caption below photo reads '"Mermaid" by Duffy Tomei. Actually the Naze Tower'. No handwritten page number visible.

pages.full_text:
art deco houses, lots of them. lovely. But glad we don't have a flat roof.
 We found a space on West Mersea Quay, [?Rothari] Park 4 Night. It's a beautiful spot. High tide almost reaches us. Lovely sunset, misty sunrise.

__Tuesday__
We had a leisurely walk in the evening going south along the sea front & in the morning we went north & inland to see the church. St Peter & Paul. 3 keyholes in the large wooden box. Beautiful tapestry kneelers, from all local organisations – darts team, play groups.
   We stopped at the Duke's Restaurant, a [?fish] bar, & had a delicious fish platter outside Smoked salmon, smoked mackerel, shrimps, [?ocean] ocean sticks, crayfish tails, dressed crab, whelk prawns, mussels, bread, salad.
   Then off to 5 lakes golfing hotel, where [?Sum] has been asked to repair bedside lights. Only 20 lights have broken out of 270 in 20 years.
   We arrived at Markes Farm, Tillingham &
pages.notes: Text at top is continuation from left page. '[?Rothari]' is uncertain — could be a campsite/park name; 'Rothari' or similar word difficult to read clearly. '[?Sum]' is a partial name — possibly a person's name (e.g. 'Simon' or similar) whose identity is unclear. 'whelk' appears on same line as 'dressed crab' — 'whelk prawns' may be two separate items 'whelks' and 'prawns'. Text ends mid-sentence continuing to next spread. No handwritten page number visible.
